name: OSMnx
role: Pull the San Francisco drivable road network from OpenStreetMap as a graph — the canonical map for training (kinematic env drives on it), routing/rewards, and the demo geometry.
decision: Keep. Use the v2.x API. Load once at init; cache to graphml so you don't re-hit Overpass every run.
license: MIT (library code) / ODbL (the OSM data it returns — must attribute OpenStreetMap in any published derivative)
inputs: place name e.g. 'San Francisco, California, USA'
or bbox tuple (west, south, east, north)
outputs: NetworkX MultiDiGraph: nodes = intersections/dead-ends, edges = street segments
edge attrs: osmid, highway, name, oneway, reversed, length(m), geometry; lanes/maxspeed when tagged (often sparse)
api_notes: v2 breaking change: ox.graph_from_bbox takes a SINGLE bbox tuple, not 4 args (most common copy-paste bug from 1.x tutorials).
Functions are flat in ox.* namespace (ox.graph_from_place, ox.add_edge_speeds, ox.features_from_point).
lanes/maxspeed are frequently missing in real OSM — impute, don't assume.
alternatives: raw Overpass API (lower-level, OSMnx wraps it)
risks: lanes/maxspeed sparsity -> impute for realistic speeds
Overpass public endpoint rate limits -> cache the graphml locally
ODbL attribution on anything you publish
